questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the city of Amritsar located?
questions.explanation: Amritsar is a major city in the state of Punjab in northern India.

questions.q: Which famous Sikh shrine is located in Amritsar?
questions.explanation: Amritsar is home to the Golden Temple, the holiest shrine of Sikhism.

questions.q: Which 1919 massacre made Amritsar a focal point of the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre of 1919 occurred in Amritsar and galvanized nationwide protest.

questions.q: In which year did the Jallianwala Bagh massacre take place in Amritsar?
questions.explanation: The massacre at Jallianwala Bagh in Amritsar took place in 1919.

questions.q: The Golden Temple in Amritsar is the holiest shrine of which religion?
questions.explanation: The Golden Temple, or Harmandir Sahib, is the central shrine of Sikhism.

questions.q: Jallianwala Bagh, the site of the 1919 massacre, is best described as a what?
questions.explanation: Jallianwala Bagh was an enclosed public garden in Amritsar where the crowd had gathered.

questions.q: Amritsar lies in which broad region of the Indian subcontinent?
questions.explanation: Amritsar is in Punjab, in the north-western part of India.

questions.q: The name 'Amritsar' is associated with which feature linked to the Golden Temple?
options: A sacred water tank (pool of nectar)
questions.explanation: The name Amritsar derives from the Amrit Sarovar, the sacred pool surrounding the Golden Temple.

questions.q: What kind of crowd was fired upon at Jallianwala Bagh in Amritsar in 1919?
questions.explanation: British troops fired on an unarmed crowd peacefully gathered at Jallianwala Bagh.

questions.q: The events in Amritsar in 1919 intensified the Indian demand for what?
questions.explanation: The massacre deepened anger against colonial rule and strengthened the demand for freedom.

questions.q: On what date in 1919 did the Jallianwala Bagh massacre occur in Amritsar?
questions.explanation: The massacre took place on 13 April 1919, the day of the Baisakhi festival.

questions.q: Which British officer ordered the firing at Jallianwala Bagh in Amritsar?
questions.explanation: Brigadier-General Reginald Dyer ordered troops to open fire on the crowd.

questions.q: The Jallianwala Bagh gathering in Amritsar coincided with which festival?
questions.explanation: The crowd had assembled on Baisakhi, a major spring festival in Punjab.

questions.q: Whose arrest and deportation helped spark unrest in Amritsar before the massacre?
questions.explanation: The arrest of local leaders Dr Saifuddin Kitchlew and Dr Satyapal stirred protest in Amritsar.

questions.q: Which honour did Rabindranath Tagore renounce in protest after the Amritsar massacre?
questions.explanation: Tagore returned his knighthood in protest against the Jallianwala Bagh massacre.

questions.q: By what name is the enclosed garden where the 1919 Amritsar massacre took place known?
questions.explanation: The massacre occurred at Jallianwala Bagh, an enclosed garden in the heart of Amritsar.

questions.q: What restrictive measure was in force in Amritsar at the time of the 1919 gathering?
options: A nationwide curfew lifted that day
questions.explanation: Dyer had banned public meetings, and the assembly defied that prohibition.

questions.q: The committee set up by the British to inquire into the Amritsar massacre was known as the what?
questions.explanation: The Hunter Commission was appointed to investigate the Jallianwala Bagh massacre.

questions.q: After the Amritsar massacre, Dyer enforced a notorious 'crawling order' on which street?
options: The road to the Golden Temple
A lane where a British woman had been assaulted
questions.explanation: Dyer ordered Indians to crawl along the lane where missionary Marcella Sherwood had been attacked.

questions.q: Which broader province was placed under martial law following the Amritsar events of 1919?
questions.explanation: Martial law was imposed across Punjab in the aftermath of the Amritsar disturbances.

questions.q: The agitation that brought crowds onto Amritsar's streets in early 1919 was directed against which law?
questions.explanation: Protests against the repressive Rowlatt Act fuelled the unrest in Amritsar before the massacre.

questions.q: Approximately how long did Dyer's troops fire on the crowd at Jallianwala Bagh in Amritsar?
questions.explanation: The troops fired for roughly ten minutes, until much of their ammunition was spent.

questions.q: Roughly how many rounds did Dyer's troops fire at Jallianwala Bagh in Amritsar?
questions.explanation: The official Hunter Commission recorded that about 1,650 rounds were fired into the crowd.

questions.q: Which young revolutionary later assassinated Michael O'Dwyer, the Punjab Lieutenant-Governor linked to the Amritsar massacre?
questions.explanation: Udham Singh shot Michael O'Dwyer in London in 1940 in revenge for the Amritsar massacre.

questions.q: In which London hall did Udham Singh shoot Michael O'Dwyer in 1940?
questions.explanation: Udham Singh shot O'Dwyer at Caxton Hall in London in March 1940.

questions.q: Which Lieutenant-Governor of Punjab approved of Dyer's actions at Amritsar?
questions.explanation: Michael O'Dwyer, Lieutenant-Governor of Punjab, endorsed Dyer's conduct at Amritsar.

questions.q: What was the official Hunter Commission figure for those killed at Jallianwala Bagh in Amritsar?
questions.explanation: The official toll was put at 379 dead, though Indian estimates ranged much higher.

questions.q: Which feature of Jallianwala Bagh in Amritsar made escape from the firing especially difficult?
options: It was surrounded by a moat
It had only narrow exits, mostly blocked by troops
questions.explanation: The garden was enclosed by buildings with only a few narrow exits, which Dyer's troops blocked.

questions.q: Many people in Amritsar's Jallianwala Bagh died by jumping into what to escape the bullets?
questions.explanation: A number of victims jumped into a well in the garden, which became a memorial site.

questions.q: Following the inquiry into Amritsar, what happened to Brigadier-General Dyer's army career?
options: He was relieved of command and forced to resign
He was knighted by the King
He was given a new command in India
questions.explanation: Dyer was censured by the Hunter Commission, removed from command, and compelled to resign.
